EAM310 · Week 2 Practice Quiz

Four Pillars · Eight Principles · Tongue & Pulse · Summer 2026
Prof. Daniel Schrier, D.O.M., L.Ac. · Quiz 2 Prep
7 Multiple Choice6 True/False 4 Matching SetsImage ID
Section A · Multiple Choice — Circle the single best answer
1.MC All of the following are true statements about the Tongue Coat (苔 Tāi) EXCEPT:
A.Reflects the state of the Yang organs, especially the Stomach
B.A thick coat indicates the presence of a pathogenic factor
C.Absence of coating indicates Yin deficiency
D.Reflects the state of Blood and Nutritive Qi at the Ying level
2.MC All of the following are correct seasonal pulse pairings EXCEPT:
A.Spring — Wiry pulse
B.Summer — Flooding pulse
C.Autumn — Stone-like / Deep pulse
D.Winter — Stone-like / Deep pulse
3.MC The following signs and symptoms are of which condition?
Red tongue body, NO coating, thready rapid pulse, low-grade fever in the afternoon, night sweats, five-palm heat, dry mouth worse at night
A.Full Heat (Excess Yang)
B.Empty Heat (Yin deficiency)
C.Full Cold (Excess Yin)
D.Wind-Heat invasion
4.MC A patient has a floating pulse with night sweats, five-palm heat, malar flush, and dry throat worse at night. This floating pulse most likely indicates:
A.Exterior Wind-Cold invasion
B.Exterior Wind-Heat invasion
C.Yin deficiency with Empty Heat
D.Yang deficiency
5.MC A patient's tongue shows peeled coating patches but has NO other signs of Yin deficiency. The most likely cause is:
A.Corticosteroid use
B.Antibiotic use
C.Stomach-Yin deficiency
D.Pepto-Bismol
6.MC What MOST reliably distinguishes Full Cold from Empty Cold?
A.Face color (bright-white vs dull-white)
B.Tongue coat thickness
C.Response to pressure (worse vs better with pressure)
D.Presence of sweating
7.MC Which system of pattern differentiation is the FOUNDATION applicable in every case regardless of which tradition is used?
A.Zang-Fu organ differentiation
B.Five Elements (五行 Wǔ Xíng)
C.Eight Principles (八纲 Bā Gāng)
D.Six Stages (Liù Jīng)
Section B · True / False — Circle True or False
8.T/F Hearing and smelling are counted as two separate pillars in the Four Pillars 四診, giving a total of five diagnostic methods.
TRUEFALSE
9.T/F A floating pulse always indicates an Exterior pattern.
TRUEFALSE
10.T/F The tongue body color is the single most reliable indicator of the true underlying condition.
TRUEFALSE
11.T/F The wiry pulse is the normal seasonal pulse variation associated with Spring in a healthy individual.
TRUEFALSE
12.T/F In a Full Cold pattern, pain is worse with pressure; in an Empty Cold pattern, pain is better with pressure.
TRUEFALSE
13.T/F Pepto-Bismol can cause a black tongue that looks pathological but is harmless and temporary.
TRUEFALSE
Section C · Matching — Write the correct letter in each blank
14.MATCH Match each season to its normal seasonal pulse quality (Huang Di Nei Jing):
___Spring 春
___Summer 夏
___Autumn 秋
___Winter 冬
AFlooding / Overflowing 洪 Hóng
BStone-like / Deep 石 Shí
CHair-like / Floating 毛 Máo
DWiry 弦 Xián
15.MATCH Match each medication to its specific effect on the tongue:
___Antibiotics
___Corticosteroids
___Pepto-Bismol
___Anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)
___Antineoplastic / chemotherapy
ABlack tongue
BRed points on tongue surface; thin body
CVery thick brown or black dry coating
DRed, swollen tongue; tip much redder
EPeeled patches — areas without coating
16.MATCH Match the pulse position to the correct organ (Modern TCM — Nanjing):
___Cun (Front) — Left wrist
___Guan (Middle) — Left wrist
___Chi (Rear) — Left wrist
___Cun (Front) — Right wrist
___Guan (Middle) — Right wrist
___Chi (Rear) — Right wrist
ALiver / Gallbladder
BSpleen / Stomach
CKidney — Yin
DHeart / Pericardium
ELung / Large Intestine
FKidney — Yang
17.MATCH Match each clinical presentation to the correct Eight Principles pattern:
___Whole face red, high fever all day, pain worse with pressure, thick yellow coat, full-rapid-overflowing pulse
___Malar flush, afternoon fever, night sweats, red tongue NO coating, floating-empty thready rapid pulse
___Cold limbs, weakness, pain improved with heat, pale tongue, slow pulse — from external Cold invasion
___Feeling cold, desiring warmth, pain improves with heat AND pressure, pale tongue, weak-slow-deep pulse
AFull Cold (Excess Yin)
BEmpty Cold (Yang deficiency)
CFull Heat (Excess Yang)
DEmpty Heat (Yin deficiency)
Section D · Image Identification — Label each zone
18.IMAGE ID The tongue map below shows five diagnostic zones marked A–E. Write the correct organ system for each zone. (Organs to place: Heart · Lung · Liver/Gallbladder · Spleen/Stomach · Kidney/Bladder/Intestines)
A
B
C
D
E

Prof. Schrier: "I will give you something like this on a quiz question. These are things you DO need to know."

Zone A =
Zone B =
Zone C =
Zone D =
Zone E =

🙈 Answer Key — No Peeking Until You've Tried Everything

1. Coat EXCEPT:
D is WRONG — That describes the tongue BODY. Coat = Yang organs / pathogenic factors (most superficial). Body = Yin organs, Blood, Nutritive Qi (Ying level).
2. Seasonal pulses EXCEPT:
C is WRONG — Autumn = Hair-like/Floating, NOT Stone-like/Deep. Winter = Stone-like/Deep. Spring=Wiry · Summer=Flooding · Autumn=Hair-like · Winter=Stone-like/Deep.
3. Red body + NO coat + thready rapid:
B — Empty Heat (Yin deficiency). Body color = true condition (red = Heat). No coat = Yin def (Stomach-Yin failing to steam coat). Afternoon fever + night sweats + five-palm heat = Empty Heat time pattern. Full Heat would have thick yellow coat + forceful pulse.
4. Floating + Yin def signs:
C — Yin deficiency with Empty Heat. Yin can't anchor Yang → pulse floats. Night sweats, five-palm heat, malar flush, dry throat at night = Yin def constellation. NOT exterior.
5. Peeled patches + no Yin def:
B — Antibiotic use. If no Yin def signs → ask about meds. "Are you on antibiotics?" Instant clinical credibility.
6. Full Cold vs Empty Cold:
C — Pressure response. Full Cold = worse with pressure (excess resists). Empty Cold = better with pressure and warmth (deficiency wants support). This is the universal Excess/Deficiency differentiator.
7. Foundation:
C — Eight Principles 八纲. Applicable in every case regardless of tradition. All other systems build on it.
8. Hearing + smelling = two pillars:
FALSE — ONE pillar (聞 Wén). Total = four, not five.
9. Floating = always exterior:
FALSE — Also = Yin deficiency. Always look at the full constellation.
10. Body color = most reliable:
TRUE — From Schrier's slides: "The tongue body color always shows the true condition."
11. Wiry = Spring normal:
TRUE — Expected seasonal variation. Does not mean Liver disease in a healthy person in spring.
12. Full Cold = worse with pressure:
TRUE — Full Cold: worse (excess resists). Empty Cold: better (deficiency wants support). This statement is correct.
13. Pepto = black tongue:
TRUE — Bismuth + oral sulfur. Harmless. Ask immediately.
14. Seasonal pulses:
Spring = D · Summer = A · Autumn = C · Winter = B
15. Medications:
Antibiotics = E · Corticosteroids = D · Pepto-Bismol = A · NSAIDs = B · Chemo = C
16. Pulse positions:
Cun-L = D (HT/PC) · Guan-L = A (LV/GB) · Chi-L = C (KD-Yin) · Cun-R = E (LU/LI) · Guan-R = B (SP/ST) · Chi-R = F (KD-Yang)
17. Pattern matching:
Whole face red + thick yellow coat = C (Full Heat) · Malar flush + no coat = D (Empty Heat) · Cold limbs + external Cold = A (Full Cold) · Better with pressure and warmth = B (Empty Cold)
18. Tongue zones:
A = Heart (tip) · B = Lung (just behind tip) · C = Liver/Gallbladder (sides) · D = Spleen/Stomach (center oval) · E = Kidney/Bladder/Intestines (root/back)
EAM310 Week 2 Practice Quiz · Prof. Daniel Schrier, D.O.M., L.Ac. · Jonathan Centeno · VUIM DAcHM · Summer 2026
